author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
This reminds me of your video on how to make acordian folds out of common goods. Would a v-shape bit of builders board with acordian folds work for a VAWT rotor?

When the wind is blowing with the the wind catchers they will expand like a sail, and the wind blowing against them would collapse them, reducing air resistance.

Care to take that idea for a walk and see how far it goes mate?

author: @normanblow
text:
Robert, when I was a young man, there was a citizen in our town that developed the guidance system for ICBMs.  At some point, he threw away his career and moved to our town and bought an old abandoned farmhouse.  He lived there with no power company electricity, but he had plenty of power.  
On top of his house, he had a sail turbine, and the sails were square but folded along the axis like triangles.  The sails caught the wind behind them and opened up and drove the shaft.  As the sails faced the wind, they folded down and presented no resistance.
I know this because he helped me build my first transmitter from an old army surplus signal generator powered by 6C4 vacuum tubes.
His whole house was lit with flourescent lights powered by his battery bank supplied by his turbine.  It was quite a sight to see in operation
Thank you for what you do :)

author: @historyisfake9153
text:
Have you seen the channel PMMG4HYBRID before? 
He is like you experimenting with free energy devices and made a self propelled motor and even rigged them up direct to an alternator and he has one that he made with the alternator built in.

PLEASE go see what he has if you find this interesting. He has an online shop but although probably worth it his prices are very high and you have to assemble them which at that price the complication would make me to worried to turn it on and burn my money.
But he is an asset to the free energy world.
I bet theres many others out there like him and I am sure in time these people will be well known and gather together to make more advancements. 

Also I paused your video to work out how sails are turning and not staying static I figured out that the one in the wind is helping the other to turn and as soon as I worked it out and clicked play you instantly went in to how it works.Mad timing!!!
Also how come the old spinning signs outside shops carries on spinning without stopping?? And did they have much torque?

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text:
My Mom had a rotating laundry line.  Square umbrella-like canopy with a stand from piece of  pipe loose fit into a larger one that was set in concrete and buried in the ground.  If a storm came up (in tornado country) when laundry was out, it would spin pretty well.
Fun experiement Rob, and good video!
Suggest running your tea towel "booms" biased outward on a chord, rather than directly intersecting into the mast rotor.
